assessment.topology: multi-component CLI/runtime with local HTTP, WebSocket, proxy, subprocess, and browser-client boundaries; not a deployed microservice stack
heuristics_triggered: knowledge-base Architecture Model names multiple runtime components/processes (CLI, app/theme/store local servers, UI extension dev console, reverse proxy, cloudflared subprocess)
packages/* contains independent package entry points (shopify CLI, create-app, plugin packages, browser dev console package)
runtime code contains local HTTP/WebSocket/proxy client calls between browser clients, local dev servers, and user-app processes
assessment.no_findings_reason: No new cross-service-only taint issue was identified beyond already drafted P4/P5/P8 local-dev findings; out-of-repo user app and Shopify platform consumers were recorded as coverage/exclusion edges rather than drafted findings.

services.name: ui-extensions-dev-console
services.process: browser client served by extension dev server

services.name: ui-extensions-server-kit-client
services.process: browser library used by dev console

services.name: graphiql-browser-client
services.process: browser client served by GraphiQL dev server

services.name: app-reverse-proxy
services.process: local reverse proxy created by app dev

services.name: app-webhook-trigger
services.process: CLI command path that emits test webhooks

edges.data_shape: WebSocket JSON messages {event: update|dispatch|log, data: ...} sent by the dev console or any browser/tunnel client that can reach /extensions.
edges.sanitization_at_boundary: URL equality only; no Origin/key/session check. Update messages check app.apiKey only when an app object is present; extension-only updates are accepted.
edges.attacker_control: yes when a malicious browser origin or tunnel visitor can connect; corroborated by P8-001 and P5-001.
edges.trust_tagged: server treats the WebSocket channel as a trusted dev console channel after path match

edges.data_shape: Connected/update/dispatch JSON carrying app metadata, store FQDN, extension payloads, asset URLs, and localization blobs.
edges.sanitization_at_boundary: client catches JSON parse failures and filters by surface; no schema validation or URL allowlist is applied in the browser client. Server payload source is local app config/build output plus updates from E001.
edges.attacker_control: indirect via E001 if the extension WebSocket is reachable to an attacker; otherwise local project/config controlled by developer repository.
edges.trust_tagged: browser client trusts the colocated dev server as authoritative for extension state
edges.notes: Potential downstream effects are consequences of E001 and are not duplicated as a separate P9 draft.

edges.data_shape: Browser-provided GraphQL JSON body, custom headers, and api_version query; status polling requests.
edges.sanitization_at_boundary: GraphQL POST/UI routes check the per-run key before proxying; /graphiql/status is not key-gated. api_version is passed to adminUrl without an observed allowlist at this boundary.
edges.attacker_control: yes if the key is known/exposed or for /status without key; corroborated by P4/P5 GraphiQL findings.

consumer_sinks.pattern: const graphqlUrl = adminUrl(storeFqdn, query.api_version as string)

edges.trust_tagged: POST is treated as trusted after key match; status route is implicitly trusted local-only

producer.pattern: return fetch(graphqlUrl, { method: 'POST', headers, body: reqBody })

consumer.pattern: Shopify Admin OAuth / Admin GraphQL endpoints

edges.data_shape: Token-bearing OAuth and Admin GraphQL requests derived from local dev session plus browser GraphiQL body.
edges.sanitization_at_boundary: Bearer/Admin access token added by GraphiQL server; custom request headers filtered by filterCustomHeaders; destination host fixed to storeFqdn/adminUrl.
edges.attacker_control: GraphQL query body can be browser-controlled after key gate; destination is Shopify platform, not in-repo.

consumer.pattern: developer app web/backend process selected by app dev rules

edges.data_shape: Raw HTTP request method/path/body/headers and WebSocket upgrades from browser/tunnel visitors, forwarded to localhost user app ports.
edges.sanitization_at_boundary: path-prefix routing only; targets are generated as localhost ports for app web/extension processes in setup-dev-processes.ts. Unmatched HTTP returns 500; unmatched WS is destroyed.
edges.attacker_control: yes for any browser/tunnel requester reaching the reverse proxy; consumer code is user project and not available in this repository.

consumer.pattern: local webhook endpoint specified by --address / prompt

edges.data_shape: POST JSON webhook sample body and JSON-parsed headers returned by Shopify sendSampleWebhook for localhost delivery.
edges.sanitization_at_boundary: address/method compatibility is validated by trigger-flags.ts; headers are parsed with JSON.parse and forwarded. Local receiver validation is out of repo.
edges.attacker_control: address is CLI/user-controlled; sample payload/headers come from Shopify platform response for the chosen topic/api version.
edges.trust_tagged: the CLI assumes the configured localhost address is the intended app receiver

producer.pattern: return fetch(url, { method: event.method, body, redirect: 'manual', headers: finalHeaders })

edges.data_shape: Theme-dev browser request path/query/body/headers forwarded to fixed Shopify storeFqdn or cdn.shopify.com with theme dev cookies/token.
edges.sanitization_at_boundary: destination host is fixed to ctx.session.storeFqdn or cdn.shopify.com and hostname mismatch is rejected; hop-by-hop/CORS headers stripped; Authorization only added for theme-dev context.
edges.attacker_control: browser/tunnel path and headers influence request path and forwarded headers; host is constrained.
edges.trust_tagged: external platform enforces final authorization; local proxy protects host selection

coverage_gaps.reason: Out-of-repo user app receivers/handlers are dynamic and not analyzable in this repository.
External Shopify platform/Admin/CDN consumers are outside P9 drafting scope unless reflected back into in-repo services.

No in-repo gRPC/proto services, Kafka/SQS/Rabbit/NATS/Redis queues, Celery/Bull workers, or shared application database topology were found. Pub/Sub strings are webhook address validation only, not in-repo publishers/consumers.
Local credential/session files are shared by CLI packages but are not independent service/database write-driven flows; they remain covered by earlier token/storage phases.
